channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Top Republicans Express Concern Over Trump Plan to Withdraw Troops From Germany
item.description: Two senior Republican lawmakers voiced concern about the Pentagon decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany, highlighting bipartisan worries about the impact on European security.

item.title: Airlines Scramble to Help Stranded Spirit Passengers After Budget Carrier Collapses
item.description: United, Delta, JetBlue and Southwest are capping ticket prices for Spirit customers who need to rebook canceled flights after the budget airline ceased operations on May 2, 2026.

item.title: NATO seeks clarification from US on Germany troop withdrawal
item.description: NATO is working with the United States to understand details of the planned drawdown of 5,000 US troops from Germany, a spokesperson said, as the alliance seeks clarity on implications for European security.

item.title: US and Philippines deploy anti-ship missiles near Taiwan in war games
item.description: Philippine and US forces showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes province during annual military exercises, highlighting escalating tensions in the Taiwan Strait as China claims the self-governed island.

item.title: OPEC+ agrees to raise oil output targets despite Hormuz disruption
item.description: OPEC+ has agreed in principle to raise oil output targets in June, but the increase will remain largely theoretical as the US-Iran war continues to disrupt Gulf oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: China Rejects US Sanctions on Refineries Over Iran Oil Links
item.description: Beijing's commerce ministry declared that China will not comply with US sanctions against five companies targeted for purchasing Iranian oil, escalating economic tensions between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: Hezbollah's Fiber-Optic Drones Pose Challenge for Israel
item.description: The militant group is using cheap drones linked to their launch site by thin fiber-optic cables that can stretch for dozens of kilometers, making them difficult to detect and jam.

item.title: Mali Probes Soldiers Over Collaboration with Jihadists and Rebels
item.description: Malian authorities are investigating soldiers for allegedly collaborating with al-Qaeda-linked militants and separatist rebels who carried out coordinated attacks that killed dozens last week.

item.title: Former F1 driver and Paralympic champion Alex Zanardi dies at 59
item.description: The Italian racing driver, who won two Champ Car titles and later became a Paralympic gold medalist after losing both legs in a crash, has died at 59.

item.title: Iran War Could Leave Trump Worse Off Than Before
item.description: After two months of conflict with no decisive outcome, the Iran standoff risks becoming a prolonged crisis that undermines Trump politically and economically.

item.title: Germany Reacts to US Troop Withdrawal Amid Iran War Strain
item.description: Germany's defense minister said the withdrawal of 5,000 US troops was foreseeable and should spur Europe to take more responsibility for its own defense.

item.title: Europe Rattled by Trump's Decision to Pull 5,000 Troops from Germany
item.description: European allies expressed alarm at the timing of the US troop withdrawal, which comes as NATO faces challenges from Russia and instability in the Middle East.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Encourages Wealthy US Catholics to Keep Donating
item.description: Pope Leo XIV on Saturday encouraged some of the wealthiest US Catholics to continue supporting his charitable works during a meeting at the Vatican.

item.title: Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi Hospitalized in Iran After Cardiac Crisis
item.description: The imprisoned Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner was rushed to intensive care after suffering a severe cardiac crisis. Her family and supporters are calling for her release.

item.title: Saudi Oil Prince's Iron Grip Faces Test After UAE's Shock OPEC Exit
item.description: Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman now faces an OPEC challenge on top of the largest ever disruption to global oil supplies after the UAE's surprise departure from the cartel.

item.title: Iran FA President Says He Was Allowed to Enter Canada, Chose to Turn Back
item.description: Mehdi Taj, president of Iran's football federation, said he could have entered Canada for FIFA Congress but chose to return after complications over his visa status.

item.title: Yemen Says Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Shabwa Coast, Heading to Somalia
item.description: The Togolese-flagged oil tanker Eureka was seized off Yemen's Shabwa province after armed individuals boarded it and redirected it toward Somali waters.

item.title: India's Logistics Pact with Russia Signals Arctic Footprint Ambitions
item.description: India's new logistics agreement with Russia aims to secure New Delhi a strategic presence in the resource-rich Arctic as climate change opens new shipping routes.

item.title: Taiwan Opposition Leader Eyes US Trip in June After Beijing Gains
item.description: KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun will test her cross-strait message when she meets think tanks and policymakers in the US, following a landmark meeting with China's Xi Jinping.

item.title: Iran War Ceasefire Negotiations Continue as Trump Rejects Latest Proposal
item.description: A ceasefire remains in place but uncertainty persists as the US and Iran negotiate through intermediaries, with Trump rejecting Tehran's latest peace proposal.

item.title: Iran War: Two Months In, No Clear Winner
item.description: After two months of conflict with Iran, the US faces an unresolved war with no decisive military or diplomatic victory, raising questions about Trump's strategy.

item.title: US Warns Shipping Firms Over Iran "Toll" Payments in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US has warned global shipping companies they could face sanctions if they pay Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, adding to the maritime blockade tensions.

item.title: US Steps in After Spirit Airlines Shutdown
item.description: The US government announced relief measures after Spirit Airlines ceased operations, stranding thousands of passengers and raising concerns about competition in the airline industry.

item.title: Pentagon Confirms Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ordered the withdrawal of about 5,000 US troops from Germany within the next 6-12 months, returning troop levels to pre-2022 numbers before Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Germany's Defense Minister Calls US Troop Drawdown Foreseeable
item.description: Boris Pistorius said Germany had anticipated the US withdrawal and that it should serve as a wake-up call for Europe to strengthen its own defense capabilities.
